oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Hernia repairs in the barn
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: O'Leary, Rachel
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: hernia
calf
bovine
surgery
umbilical
scrotal
wrap
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Hernias in calves are often treatable diseases. Umbilical her­nias usually present at birth, are hereditary and are considered elective procedures to correct. Umbilical hernias may also oc­cur secondary to trauma or infection. Scrotal hernias may pres­ent later on due to trauma or environmental factors and are an emergency surgery. Depending on the calf, hernia size, and hernia location, reducing and wrapping the hernia may be a non-invasive option. If the hernia is unable to be managed with wrapping or fails to resolve with wrapping, surgical techniques can be utilized to reduce the hernia.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 138-142
